title: Forced-Crisp Syrian Rhubarb Tagine
description: A flavorful and tangy Syrian dish made with forced rhubarb, pickles, and a blend of aromatic spices. Perfect for a weeknight dinner.

ingredients.name: Pickle Juice
ingredients.notes: Chop the pickles and reserve for later use

ingredients.name: Ghee
ingredients.notes: Melted or softened for the tagine

ingredients.name: Turmeric
ingredients.notes: Ground turmeric for a hint of earthiness

ingredients.name: Cinnamon
ingredients.notes: Ground cinnamon for a touch of sweetness

ingredients.name: Chopped Pickles
ingredients.notes: Reserve the pickle juice for later use

instructions: Heat ghee in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add cumin, coriander, turmeric, and cinnamon. Cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add forced rhubarb and cook for 4-5 minutes, until slightly tender.
Add pickle juice, salt, and chopped pickles. Stir to combine.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes.
Garnish with chopped parsley and serve hot.
Serve over cauliflower rice or with a side of steamed vegetables, if desired.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
